body.title: Installer device is the owner of global instance-init (global_kb + bootstrap)
body.text:
# D-installer-absorbs-init-2026-06-28
**title:** Installer device is the owner of global instance-init
**date:** 2026-06-28
**status:** open
**spawned_tickets:** T-installer-init-intention-point, T-home-global-kb-under-installer, T-inventory-classify-scattered-bootstraps, T-consequence-installer-absorbs-init

## Decision narrative
There was no defined 'init container': global instance-init (seed the shared KB, create shared tables, provision a fresh instance) was ownerless and scattered (global_kb.py + dead palace/search bootstraps + igor-local bootstraps), with no architecture intention-point. Akien (2026-06-28) decided the INSTALLER DEVICE absorbs global init — 'stand up a fresh instance' already is its job (factory, manifest, deploy), and seeding/table-creation is the same lifecycle phase. KEY BOUNDARY (prevents over-pulling): the installer owns GLOBAL / cross-instance init only; DEVICE-LOCAL bootstrap (a device standing up its own state, e.g. igor/acquisition/bootstrap.py, igor/setup_assets/installer.py, igor/tools/bootstrap_reader.py) STAYS with its device to preserve blast-radius containment. global_kb is global -> moves under installer. The other scattered pieces get inventoried+classified before any move (bootstrap_learning.py is the ambiguous one). This SUPERSEDES the 'global_kb left as-is' note in D-root-cleanup-2026-06-28 — global_kb is NOT left at root; it homes under installer. Owner: CC.

## Hypothesis
The installer device has an architecture intention-point declaring it the global-init owner; global_kb (loader + seed) lives under the installer device; and a classified inventory of the remaining scattered bootstraps (global vs device-local) exists.

## Measurement Signal
ls unseen_university/devices/installer/ shows the global_kb loader+seed; devlab/runtime/memory/architecture/installer.json exists naming the global-vs-device-local boundary; grep finds no unseen_university/global_kb.py at the old path; the bootstrap inventory note tags each piece global/device-local.

## Intention
I intend that there is one clear owner for global instance-init — the installer device — so seed/bootstrap logic has a home instead of scattering ownerless across the tree.
